title: Standardization Agreement

infobox.value: (**STANAG**, [redundantly](/wiki/Redundant_acronym "Redundant acronym"): **STANAG agreement**) defines processes, procedures, terms, and conditions for common [military](/wiki/Military "Military") or technical procedures or equipment between the member countries of the alliance. Each NATO state ratifies a STANAG and implements it within its own military. The purpose is to provide common operational and administrative procedures and [logistics](/wiki/Logistics "Logistics"), so one member nation's military may use the stores and support of another member's military.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Standardization Agreement](/w/index.php?title=Standardization_Agreement&redirect=no "Standardization Agreement"))

NATO technical standard (STANAG)

This article is about the list of NATO agreements. For the specific agreement regarding gun magazines, see [STANAG magazine](/wiki/STANAG_magazine "STANAG magazine").

In [NATO](/wiki/NATO "NATO"), a **standardization agreement** (**STANAG**, [redundantly](/wiki/Redundant_acronym "Redundant acronym"): **STANAG agreement**) defines processes, procedures, terms, and conditions for common [military](/wiki/Military "Military") or technical procedures or equipment between the member countries of the alliance. Each NATO state ratifies a STANAG and implements it within its own military. The purpose is to provide common operational and administrative procedures and [logistics](/wiki/Logistics "Logistics"), so one member nation's military may use the stores and support of another member's military.
STANAGs also form the basis for technical interoperability between a wide variety of communication and information systems (CIS) essential for NATO and Allied operations.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The Allied Data Publication 34 (ADatP-34) [NATO Interoperability Standards and Profiles](/wiki/NATO_Interoperability_Standards_and_Profiles "NATO Interoperability Standards and Profiles") which is covered by STANAG 5524, maintains a catalogue of relevant [information and communication technology](/wiki/Information_and_communications_technology "Information and communications technology") standards.

STANAGs are published in [English](/wiki/English_language "English language") and [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"), the two official [languages](/wiki/Language "Language") of NATO, by the [NATO Standardization Office](/wiki/NATO_Standardization_Office "NATO Standardization Office") in [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels").

Among the hundreds of standardization agreements (the total as of April 2007[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Standardization_agreement&action=edit) was just short of 1,300) are those for [calibres](/wiki/Caliber "Caliber") of small arms [ammunition](/wiki/Ammunition "Ammunition"), map markings, communications procedures, and classification of bridges.

:   (Edition 4, 19 December 2016): NATO Digital Motion Imagery Standard

STANAG 4624
:   [30x173mm](/wiki/30_mm_caliber "30 mm caliber") autocannon ammunition

[STANAG 4626](/wiki/STANAG_4626 "STANAG 4626")
:   Modular and Open Avionics Architectures – Part I – Architecture

STANAG 4628
:   (Edition 1, 16 March 2011): Controller Area Network (Can) Protocols For Military Applications

[STANAG 4671](/wiki/NATO_STANAG_4671 "NATO STANAG 4671")
:   (Edition 3, 2 April 2019): Unmanned Aircraft Systems Airworthiness Requirements (USAR)

STANAG 4676
:   (Edition 1, 20 May 2014): NATO Intelligence Surveillance Reconnaissance Tracking Standard (NITS)

[STANAG 4694](/wiki/NATO_Accessory_Rail "NATO Accessory Rail")
:   NATO Accessory Rail

STANAG 4703
:   UAV[[15]](#cite_note-dieu1-15)

STANAG 4704
:   NATO requirements for calibration support of test & measurement equipment

STANAG 4748
:   JANUS, used for [underwater acoustic communication](/wiki/Underwater_acoustic_communication "Underwater acoustic communication")

STANAG 4754
:   [NATO Generic Vehicle Architecture](/w/index.php?title=NATO_Generic_Vehicle_Architecture&action=edit&redlink=1 "NATO Generic Vehicle Architecture (page does not exist)") (NGVA) for Land Systems

STANAG 4774
:   Confidentiality Label Syntax

STANAG 4778
:   Metadata Binding Mechanism

STANAG 4820
:   Technical performance specification providing for the interchangeability of [4.6 mm X 30](/wiki/HK_4.6%C3%9730mm "HK 4.6×30mm") ammunition

[STANAG 5518](/wiki/JREAP "JREAP")
:   Joint Range Extension Applications Protocol (JREAP), a [Tactical Data Link](/wiki/Tactical_Data_Link "Tactical Data Link") (TDL) protocol

[STANAG 5524](/wiki/NATO_Interoperability_Standards_and_Profiles "NATO Interoperability Standards and Profiles")
:   NATO Interoperability Standards and Profiles, a catalogue of relevant information and communication technology standards

[STANAG 5602](/wiki/STANAG_5602 "STANAG 5602")
:   Standard Interface for Military Platform Link Evaluation (SIMPLE), a [Tactical Data Link](/wiki/Tactical_Data_Link "Tactical Data Link") (TDL) protocol

## Drafts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Standardization_agreement&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Drafts")]

[STANAG 4179](/wiki/STANAG_4179 "STANAG 4179")
:   A type of detachable firearm magazine proposed for standardization based on the USGI M16 rifle magazine.[[16]](#cite_note-dtic.mil-16)

[STANAG 4181](/wiki/STANAG_magazine#Loading_tools "STANAG magazine")
:   A type of [stripper clip](/wiki/Stripper_clip "Stripper clip") and guide tool use to load magazines proposed for standardization based on the USGI M16 rifle stripper clips and guide tools.[[16]](#cite_note-dtic.mil-16)
